steps.title: Identify the fit between principle and example
steps.content: The passage establishes that demonstration sports showcase sports related to the host country, then provides two examples: an Austrian sport and a French sport chosen in Austria and France respectively. The key signal is that these examples perfectly embody the stated purpose.

steps.title: Match the logical fit with a transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is appropriateness or irony-free consequence. The passage states that demonstration sports were chosen to showcase sports related to the host country. The second sentence confirms that icestocksport (Austrian) was chosen at the Austria Games and canne de combat (French) at the France Games — exactly matching the stated purpose. The blank signals that this outcome is exactly what you’d expect.
  • Choice A — Fittingly,: signals that the second sentence is an apt, appropriate outcome given what was just explained. This fits perfectly — the sports were chosen precisely because they matched the host countries. ✓
  • Choice B — Nevertheless,: signals contrast despite an obstacle — the second sentence would happen in spite of something. No obstacle exists; the outcome follows the stated rule. ✗
  • Choice C — Additionally,: signals that the second sentence adds an independent new fact. But the second sentence illustrates the rule stated in the first sentence, not a new independent fact. ✗
  • Choice D — By contrast,: signals opposition. No opposition exists — the examples confirm the principle. ✗
That matches Choice A — Fittingly,.
